card.intuition: A self-balancing BST where every node's left and right subtree heights differ by at most 1 — maintained via rotations after each insert or delete.
card.analogy: A librarian who, every time they add or remove a book, checks the nearest shelves are not too lopsided and immediately reshuffles a small section to restore balance — the whole library never becomes unwieldy.
card.time_complexity: O(log n) guaranteed for search, insert, delete — no worst-case degradation
when_to_use: Frequent lookups in a dynamic sorted set where O(log n) worst case is required
Building ordered statistics structures (rank, select queries)
When Red-Black trees are considered too complex but guaranteed O(log n) is needed
gotchas: Balance factor = height(left) − height(right); rebalance when |bf| > 1
Four rotation cases: LL (right rotation), RR (left rotation), LR (left-right = left rotate child then right rotate node), RL (right-left = right rotate child then left rotate node)
After every insertion or deletion, update heights bottom-up and check balance factor at each ancestor

exercise.prompt: Complete the AVL left-rotation. After rotating, update heights of the swapped nodes.
hints: z.right = T2 — the subtree T2 (which was y's left child) becomes z's new right child after y takes z's place
Update z's height first (z is now a child of y), then update y's height — order matters because y's height depends on z's
